strategy.char_limit_note: 200 characters is an absolute hard limit on LinkedIn free accounts. Count every character including spaces and punctuation. Test in a plain-text editor before sending.
strategy.tone: Slightly awkward genuine observation — specific and imperfect. Stands out from polished AI slop by being concrete and mildly unpolished.
anti_patterns: Do not use 'I came across your profile'
Do not use 'I was impressed by' or 'I love what you do'
Do not mention AI, automation, digital marketing, web design, or SEO explicitly
Do not pitch in the note — curiosity and connection only
Do not use 'reach out', 'touch base', or 'synergies'
Do not reference the email sequence, the audit, or the SSL/mobile finding
Do not start with 'I' — start with their name or the business name
what_works: Start with their name — personal, cuts through noise
Name the specific business — shows real attention, not a mass blast
One specific observation — not vague, not flattering, just real
End with low-pressure framing: 'in case it's useful', 'connecting in case relevant'
Slightly awkward construction is fine — reads human, not bot
strategy.post_reference_priority: HANDS: always check profile for posts from the last 60 days BEFORE sending any note. Post-reference note has 3-4x higher acceptance rate than generic.
strategy.sector_post_reference_priority: Solicitors > Accountants > Estate agents > Hotels/B&Bs > Restaurants > Retail > Trades
strategy.batch_5_rule: Do NOT reference the email sequence, website audit, or any specific technical finding (SSL, mobile, keyword gap) in any LinkedIn note. Treat as a completely cold connection from someone who noticed the business through general research.

active_notes_note: Zero active notes. No linkedin_profiles.json has been received in 45 campaign sessions. Notes will be generated per-lead once HANDS returns profile data from Friday 2026-05-15 execution.

batch_5_note_templates.status: STANDBY — activates once HANDS returns linkedin_profiles.json from Friday 2026-05-15 execution
batch_5_note_templates.trigger: HANDS finds profile → writes to linkedin_profiles.json → BRAIN generates per-lead personalised note → HANDS sends connection request with note
batch_5_note_templates.hard_deadline: Tuesday 2026-05-19 (Day 7). No extensions. Archive trigger fires immediately if missed.
batch_5_note_templates.note_generation_by_brain: BRAIN generates actual note text once profile data arrives — sector, post summary, name, business. HANDS does not write notes directly.

ssl_pas.post_reference: [First name] — saw your post about [specific topic]. [Business name] caught my attention looking at [sector] businesses in [town]. Noticed something about the online presence. Connecting in case it's relevant.
ssl_pas.generic: [First name] — [Business name] came up when I was looking at [sector] businesses in [town]. Noticed something about the online presence worth a quick mention. Connecting in case it's useful.
ssl_pas.char_guidance: Generic: ~155-170 chars depending on name/business/town length. Post-reference: ~170-195 chars — count carefully.

mobile_pas.post_reference: [First name] — saw your post about [specific topic]. [Business name] caught my attention looking at [sector] in [town]. Something about how the site works on phones caught my eye. Connecting in case relevant.
mobile_pas.generic: [First name] — [Business name] came up when I was looking at [sector] in [town]. Noticed something about how the site works on phones — worth a quick mention. Connecting in case useful.
mobile_pas.char_guidance: Mobile phrasing runs slightly longer — always count. 'On phones' → 'on mobile' saves 3 chars if needed.

no_website_bab.post_reference: [First name] — saw your post about [specific topic]. [Business name] came up looking at [sector] businesses around [town]. Something about local online visibility caught my attention. Connecting in case useful.
no_website_bab.generic: [First name] — [Business name] came up when I was looking at [sector] businesses around [town]. Something about local online visibility caught my attention. Connecting in case it's worth a conversation.
no_website_bab.char_guidance: No-website notes reference 'local online visibility' rather than 'online presence' — more accurate framing for a business with no site.

aida_keyword_gap.post_reference: [First name] — saw your post about [specific topic]. [Business name] has a strong reputation around [town]. Something about how easy you are to find online caught my attention. Connecting in case it's useful.
aida_keyword_gap.generic: [First name] — [Business name] has a strong reputation around [town]. Noticed something about how easy you are to find online — worth a quick mention. Connecting in case it's useful.
aida_keyword_gap.char_guidance: High-review leads scrutinise requests more carefully. Keep well under 200 chars — aim for 160-175.
aida_keyword_gap.qualification: 4.5 stars+ AND 100+ reviews only — test batch (5% of Batch 5)

solicitors.post_likelihood: HIGH — solicitors post about legal updates, case law, local events, charity work
solicitors.example_post_reference: [First name] — saw your post about [legal topic / local event]. [Firm name] caught my attention looking at professional services in Malton. Noticed something about the firm's online presence. Connecting in case relevant.
solicitors.char_guidance: Solicitor firm names tend to be long — count carefully. Trim '[Firm name] caught my attention' → '[Firm name] stood out' to save 4 chars if needed.

estate_agents.post_likelihood: HIGH — estate agents post property market updates, area guides, sold listings
estate_agents.example_post_reference: [First name] — saw your post about [market update / local area]. [Agency name] came up when I was looking at estate agents in [town]. Noticed something about the online presence. Connecting in case useful.
estate_agents.char_guidance: Standard length — usually fits comfortably within 200 chars.

hotels_bbs.post_likelihood: MEDIUM-HIGH — hospitality owners post events, seasonal offers, local area highlights
hotels_bbs.example_post_reference: [First name] — saw your post about [event / local highlight]. [Business name] caught my attention looking at accommodation in [town]. Something about the online presence is worth mentioning. Connecting in case relevant.
hotels_bbs.char_guidance: Hospitality business names vary widely in length — count carefully.

restaurants.post_likelihood: MEDIUM-HIGH — fine dining owners post menus, seasonal specials, supplier stories
restaurants.example_post_reference: [First name] — saw your post about [menu / supplier / event]. [Restaurant name] came up looking at restaurants in [town]. Noticed something about the online presence. Connecting in case it's useful.
restaurants.char_guidance: Fine dining names in Helmsley tend to be short — usually fits well.

trades.note: Use generic note only. Do not attempt post-reference for trades. Skip LinkedIn entirely if only first name known.
trades.generic_fallback: [First name] — [Business name] came up when I was looking at [trade] businesses in [town]. Noticed something about the online presence worth a quick mention. Connecting in case it's useful.

if_too_long: Shorten town name if possible ('North Yorkshire' → omit county entirely)
'caught my attention looking at' → 'came up when I looked at' (saves ~5 chars)
'Connecting in case it's useful' → 'Connecting in case useful' (saves 3 chars)
Remove 'Connecting' opener entirely: 'Happy to share if useful' (saves more but loses the framing)

monday_fallback_notes.status: ACTIVE — use if LinkedIn was NOT executed Friday 2026-05-15
monday_fallback_notes.note: These are direct-use templates for Monday 2026-05-18 execution. HANDS fills [bracketed placeholders] from their LinkedIn search results. No further BRAIN run required to use these. 200-char hard limit — fill placeholders carefully and count before sending.
monday_fallback_notes.trigger: linkedin_profiles.json missing after Friday session → execute Monday using these templates
monday_fallback_notes.hard_deadline: Tuesday 2026-05-19 EOD — archive trigger fires for any Batch 5 lead without confirmed LinkedIn
rules: Check profile for posts from last 60 days FIRST — post-reference is strongly preferred (3-4x acceptance)
If post found: use post_reference_any_sector template below
If no post: use the sector-appropriate generic template
200 characters is a hard LinkedIn limit — count every character including spaces
Do NOT mention email, SSL, 'Not Secure', mobile, website audit, or any prior contact
Do NOT start the note with 'I'
No exclamation marks, no 'reach out', no 'synergies'
Write profiles.json entry immediately after each search — mandatory
IMAP sweep each lead before sending their note (per lead, not bulk)

post_reference_any_sector.template: [First name] — saw your post about [post topic in 4-6 words]. [One honest specific observation about the post, 5-8 words]. Good to connect. [Your first name]
post_reference_any_sector.example: James — saw your post about rural broadband in Ryedale. The infrastructure point rang true. Good to connect. Chris
post_reference_any_sector.note: This template works for any sector. Adjust the honest observation to reflect what the post actually said — do not fabricate. If the post is about something unrelated to business: 'Interesting angle on [topic]' is fine. Well within 200 chars — you have room.

solicitors_generic.template: [First name] — [Firm name] stood out looking at professional services in [town]. Good to see an established local firm. Would be good to connect. [Your first name]
solicitors_generic.example: David — Malton & Co Solicitors stood out looking at professional services in Malton. Good to see an established local firm. Would be good to connect. Chris
solicitors_generic.sector_note: Solicitors post frequently — check for posts FIRST before using this generic. Legal updates, local event support, charity involvement are common topics.

estate_agents_generic.template: [First name] — [Agency name] came up when I was looking at estate agents in [town]. Something about the local market coverage caught my attention. Good to connect. [Your first name]
estate_agents_generic.example: Andrew — Ryedale Property came up when I was looking at estate agents in Malton. Something about the local market coverage caught my attention. Good to connect. Chris
estate_agents_generic.sector_note: Estate agents post market updates and sold listings frequently — high post-reference likelihood. Check posts first.

hotels_bbs_generic.template: [First name] — [Business name] caught my eye when I was exploring [town]. Good to see independent hospitality doing well in North Yorkshire. Connecting in case useful. [Your first name]
hotels_bbs_generic.example: Sarah — The Feversham Arms caught my eye when I was exploring Helmsley. Good to see independent hospitality doing well in North Yorkshire. Connecting in case useful. Chris
hotels_bbs_generic.sector_note: Hospitality owners in Helmsley and Pickering post about seasonal events and local highlights. Helmsley: highest post-reference likelihood in the Ryedale batch.

restaurants_generic.template: [First name] — [Restaurant name] came up when I was looking at restaurants in [town]. Good to see independent places doing well in North Yorkshire. Good to connect. [Your first name]
restaurants_generic.example: Emma — The Star Inn came up when I was looking at restaurants in Harome. Good to see independent places doing well in North Yorkshire. Good to connect. Chris
restaurants_generic.sector_note: Fine dining in Helmsley (Harome is nearby) — owners post menus, seasonal ingredients, supplier stories. Post-reference strongly recommended.

trades_generic.template: [First name] — [Business name] came up when I was looking at [trade] businesses in [town]. Noticed something about the online presence. Connecting in case it's useful. [Your first name]
trades_generic.example: James — Malton Plumbing came up when I was looking at plumbing businesses in Malton. Noticed something about the online presence. Connecting in case it's useful. Chris
trades_generic.sector_note: Trades LinkedIn presence is LOW. Skip if: (1) no confirmed LinkedIn profile, (2) only first name known (no surname). Do not send to a profile you are not confident is the owner.

retail_boutique_generic.template: [First name] — [Business name] stood out when I was looking at independent retail in [town]. Great to see local shops doing well in North Yorkshire. Good to connect. [Your first name]
retail_boutique_generic.example: Helen — Malton Cheese Shop stood out when I was looking at independent retail in Malton. Great to see local shops doing well in North Yorkshire. Good to connect. Chris

if_over_200: Remove county entirely — 'North Yorkshire' is 15 chars. Use '[town]' alone instead of '[town], North Yorkshire'
'Connecting in case it's useful' → 'Good to connect' (saves 16 chars)
'when I was looking at' → 'looking at' (saves 11 chars)
Shorten the 'North Yorkshire' phrase entirely if needed
char_count_troubleshooting.note: Always count in a plain text editor before sending. LinkedIn counts characters including spaces and punctuation.

linkedin_profiles_json_schema.instruction: For each Batch 5 lead searched on Monday, add one entry to data/linkedin_profiles.json immediately after the search. Do not batch — write each entry as you go.

sector_priority.note: Highest priority — professional services always on LinkedIn
Medium-high — Helmsley hospitality owners often active
Low presence — skip if profile uncertain
